---
title: "Replication Code for Flex Paper"
author: "Jihyeon bae"
date: "June 10th 2025"
output: html_document
---

# Setup and packages

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library("pacman")
p_load(plyr, dplyr, tidyr, ggplot2, tidyverse, RColorBrewer, readxl,
       readr, haven, countrycode, clubSandwich, lmtest,plm,
       rqog, igoR, modelsummary, knitr, kableExtra, flextable, unvotes)

```

# Loading Raw data
```{r, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

igo_analysis <- readRDS( "~/Desktop/igo_mia_2026.rds")

```



## Visualization
```{r, warning=FALSE}
library(gplots)
plotmeans(poolconstit ~ year, main = "Variation across time", data = igo_analysis, 
          n.label=FALSE)
plotmeans(poolconstit ~ ioname, main = "Variation across IGOs", data = igo_analysis, 
          n.label=FALSE, 
          xlab="",
      mar=c(10.1,4.1,4.1,2.1), 
          las=2)
```

##Investating the y variable
```{r}
library(plm)
library(dplyr)

pdata <- pdata.frame(igo_analysis, index = c("cow_igocode", "year"))

# --- 1. xtsum-style decomposition ---
overall_var  <- var(igo_analysis$poolconstit, na.rm = TRUE)
between_var  <- igo_analysis %>%
  group_by(cow_igocode) %>%
  summarise(m = mean(poolconstit, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  summarise(v = var(m)) %>% pull(v)
within_var   <- igo_analysis %>%
  group_by(cow_igocode) %>%
  mutate(demeaned = poolconstit - mean(poolconstit, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  summarise(v = var(demeaned, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% pull(v)

cat("Overall variance:  ", round(overall_var, 4), "\n")
cat("Between-IGO variance:", round(between_var, 4), "\n")
cat("Within-IGO variance: ", round(within_var, 4), "\n")
cat("Within share:        ", round(within_var / overall_var * 100, 1), "%\n")

# --- 2. How many IGOs actually change poolconstit? ---
switchers <- igo_analysis %>%
  dplyr::group_by(cow_igocode) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_years      = n(),
    n_changes    = sum(abs(diff(poolconstit)) > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    ever_changed = any(abs(diff(poolconstit)) > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    range        = max(poolconstit, na.rm = TRUE) - min(poolconstit, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

cat("\nIGOs that ever changed poolconstit:", sum(switchers$ever_changed), 
    "out of", nrow(switchers), "\n")
cat("Mean within-IGO range:", round(mean(switchers$range), 3), "\n")
```


```{r}
detach("package:plyr", unload = TRUE)
library(dplyr)

# --- Priority 1: Cross-section at founding year (cleanest) ---
founding <- igo_analysis %>%
  dplyr::group_by(cow_igocode) %>%
  dplyr::slice_min(year, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

model_cs <- lm(poolconstit ~ polyarchy + gdp_cap + population +
               trade  + number + political + social +
               colonial +  hh_gdp + hh_poly + ideal_sd,
               data = founding)
coeftest(model_cs, vcov = vcovHC(model_cs, type = "HC1"))

# --- Priority 2: Between estimator (IGO-mean regression) ---
model_between <- plm(poolconstit ~ polyarchy + gdp_cap + population +
                     trade  + number + political + social +
                     colonial  + hh_gdp + hh_poly + ideal_sd,
                     data = igo_analysis,
                     index = c("cow_igocode", "year"),
                     model = "between")
summary(model_between)

# --- Priority 3: Random effects (uses both between + within) ---
model_re <- plm(poolconstit ~ polyarchy_median + gdp_cap + population +
                trade  + number + political + social +
                colonial  + hh_gdp + hh_poly + ideal_sd,
                data = igo_analysis,
                index = c("cow_igocode", "year"),
                model = "random")


# --- Priority 4: Switchers-only analysis ---
# Tests whether the within-IGO changes that DO occur follow your theory
switcher_igos <- switchers %>% 
  dplyr::filter(ever_changed == TRUE) %>% 
  dplyr::pull(cow_igocode)

model_switchers <- plm(poolconstit ~ polyarchy + gdp_cap + population +
                       trade  + number + political + social +
                       colonial  + hh_gdp + hh_poly + ideal_sd,
                       data = dplyr::filter(igo_analysis, 
                                            cow_igocode %in% switcher_igos),
                       index = c("cow_igocode", "year"),
                       model = "within", effect = "twoways")
coeftest(model_switchers, vcov = vcovBK(model_switchers, 
                                         type = "HC1", cluster = "group"))
```
# Main  Model
```{r}
# Check the actual distribution of poolconstit
table(round(igo_analysis$poolconstit, 3))

# What share is exactly zero?
mean(igo_analysis$poolconstit == 0, na.rm = TRUE)

# What are the mass points? (recall: score = voting_rule × ratification_weight)
# Possible values from your table:
# 0.33×1.00 = 0.330, 0.66×0.25 = 0.165, 0.66×0.50 = 0.330, etc.
hist(igo_analysis$poolconstit, breaks = 30, 
     main = "Distribution of Constitutional Pooling",
     xlab = "poolconstit")



y <- igo_analysis$poolconstit

model1 <- plm(y ~ polyarchy_median + gdp_cap + population + trade +
               number + political + social +
              colonial + hh_gdp + hh_poly + ideal_sd,
              data   = igo_analysis,
              index  = c("cow_igocode", "year"),
              model  = "within",
              effect = "time")

model2 <- plm(y ~ polyarchy + gdp_cap + population + trade +
               number + political + social +
              colonial + hh_gdp + hh_poly + ideal_sd,
              data   = igo_analysis,
              index  = c("cow_igocode", "year"),
              model  = "within",
              effect = "time")

model3 <- plm(y ~ percentage + gdp_cap + population + trade +
                number + political + social +
              colonial + polyarchy_sd + hh_gdp + hh_poly + ideal_sd,
              data   = igo_analysis,
              index  = c("cow_igocode", "year"),
              model  = "within",
              effect = "time")

# Results
coeftest(model1, vcov = vcovBK(model1, type = "HC1", cluster = "group"))
coeftest(model2, vcov = vcovBK(model2, type = "HC1", cluster = "group"))
coeftest(model3, vcov = vcovBK(model3, type = "HC1", cluster = "group"))

# Model without polyarchy_sd
model2_nosd <- plm(y ~ polyarchy + gdp_cap + population + trade +
                   number + political + social +
                   colonial + hh_gdp + hh_poly + ideal_sd,
                   data   = igo_analysis,
                   index  = c("cow_igocode", "year"),
                   model  = "within",
                   effect = "time")

coeftest(model2_nosd, vcov = vcovBK(model2_nosd, type="HC1", cluster="group"))





detach("package:plyr", unload = TRUE)
library(dplyr)
library(plm)
library(lmtest)
library(clubSandwich)
library(modelsummary)

y <- igo_analysis$poolconstit

# --- Block 1: Linear models (your original claim) ---
# Tests simple monotonic negative relationship

m1_linear <- plm(y ~ polyarchy_median + gdp_cap + population + trade +
                 number + political + social + colonial + 
                hh_gdp + hh_poly + ideal_sd,
                 data = igo_analysis, index = c("cow_igocode", "year"),
                 model = "within", effect = "time")

m2_linear <- plm(y ~ polyarchy + gdp_cap + population + trade +
                 number + political + social + colonial + 
                  hh_gdp + hh_poly + ideal_sd,
                 data = igo_analysis, index = c("cow_igocode", "year"),
                 model = "within", effect = "time")

m3_linear <- plm(y ~ percentage + gdp_cap + population + trade +
                 number + political + social + colonial + 
                   hh_gdp + hh_poly + ideal_sd,
                 data = igo_analysis, index = c("cow_igocode", "year"),
                 model = "within", effect = "time")

   
# --- Run all models ---
vcov_bk <- function(x) vcovBK(x, type = "HC1", cluster = "group")

# Linear block
coeftest(m1_linear, vcov = vcov_bk(m1_linear))
coeftest(m2_linear, vcov = vcov_bk(m2_linear))
coeftest(m3_linear, vcov = vcov_bk(m3_linear))



# --- Robustness: Between estimator ---
# Pure cross-sectional identification — addresses sticky DV concern
m_between <- plm(y ~ polyarchy + gdp_cap + population + trade +
                 number + political + social + colonial + 
                   hh_gdp + hh_poly + ideal_sd,
                 data = igo_analysis, index = c("cow_igocode", "year"),
                 model = "between")
summary(m_between)

# --- Robustness: Founding year cross-section ---
founding <- igo_analysis %>%
  dplyr::group_by(cow_igocode) %>%
  dplyr::slice_min(year, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

m_founding <- lm(poolconstit ~ polyarchy + gdp_cap + population + trade +
                 number + political + social + colonial + 
                   hh_gdp + hh_poly + ideal_sd,
                 data = founding)
coeftest(m_founding, vcov = vcovHC(m_founding, type = "HC1"))

```

# is AIGO more homogeneous?
```{r}
# --- Define AIGO vs DIGO based on mean polyarchy < 0.5 ---

igo_analysis <- igo_analysis %>%
  filter(is.na(polyarchy)==FALSE) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    aigo = ifelse(polyarchy < 0.5, 1, 0)
  )

# --- 1. Simple mean comparison ---
igo_analysis %>%
  dplyr::group_by(aigo) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    mean_sd   = mean(ideal_sd, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_sd = median(ideal_sd, na.rm = TRUE),
    n         = dplyr::n()
  )

# --- 2. T-test ---
t.test(polyarchy_sd ~ aigo, data = igo_analysis)

# --- 3. More rigorous: regression controlling for IGO size ---
# Larger IGOs mechanically have higher SD — need to control for this
homogeneity_model <- lm(ideal_sd ~ aigo + number + gdp_cap + 
                        population + as.factor(year),
                        data = igo_analysis)
coeftest(homogeneity_model, 
         vcov = vcovCL(homogeneity_model, cluster = ~cow_igocode))

# --- 4. Visualize the distribution ---
ggplot(igo_analysis, aes(x = ideal_sd, 
                          fill = factor(aigo,
                            labels = c("DIGO", "AIGO")))) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("grey70", "grey30")) +
  labs(x     = "Political Heterogeneity (SD of Polyarchy)",
       y     = "Density",
       fill  = "",
       title = "Regime Homogeneity: AIGOs vs. DIGOs") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "Times New Roman", size = 12))

# --- 1. Correlation between mean polyarchy and SD ---
cor.test(igo_analysis$polyarchy, igo_analysis$ideal_sd, 
         use = "complete.obs")

# --- 2. Regression: does higher polyarchy predict higher SD? ---
# Controlling for IGO size since larger IGOs mechanically have more dispersion
homogeneity_model <- lm(ideal_sd ~ polyarchy + number + 
                        as.factor(year),
                        data = igo_analysis)
coeftest(homogeneity_model,
         vcov = vcovCL(homogeneity_model, cluster = ~cow_igocode))

# --- 3. Visualize ---
ggplot(igo_analysis, aes(x = polyarchy, y = ideal_sd)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2, color = "grey50") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black", se = TRUE) +
  labs(x     = "Mean Polyarchy Score",
       y     = "Political Heterogeneity (SD of Polyarchy)", title = "IGOs and Heterogeneity") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "Times New Roman", size = 12))



m_interact <- plm(poolconstit ~ aigo * polyarchy_sd + 
                  gdp_cap + population + trade +
                  number + political + social + colonial +
                  hh_gdp + hh_poly + ideal_sd,
                  data   = igo_analysis,
                  index  = c("cow_igocode", "year"),
                  model  = "within",
                  effect = "time")

coeftest(m_interact, vcov = vcovBK(m_interact, type = "HC1", cluster = "group"))



# --- Does the polyarchy effect differ across the distribution? ---
# Split into thirds and check pooling means

igo_analysis <- igo_analysis %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    poly_tercile = dplyr::ntile(polyarchy, 3),
    poly_group   = dplyr::case_when(
      polyarchy < 0.33               ~ "AIGO (<0.33)",
      polyarchy >= 0.33 & polyarchy < 0.66 ~ "Mixed (0.33-0.66)",
      polyarchy >= 0.66              ~ "DIGO (>0.66)"
    )
  )

# Mean pooling by group
igo_analysis %>%
  dplyr::group_by(poly_group) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    mean_pooling = mean(poolconstit, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_sd      = mean(polyarchy_sd, na.rm = TRUE),
    n            = dplyr::n()
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(mean_pooling)

igo_dataset %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(ideal_mean), !is.na(polyarchy)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(igo_type = ifelse(polyarchy < 0.5, "AIGO", "DIGO")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = polyarchy, y = ideal_mean, color = igo_type)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 1.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, 
              aes(group = igo_type), color = "black") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey30") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0,   linetype = "dashed", color = "grey30") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("AIGO" = "grey20", 
                                 "DIGO" = "grey70")) +
  annotate("text", x = 0.15, y = 3,
           label = "AIGO", family = "Times New Roman", 
           size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 0.85, y = 3,
           label = "DIGO", family = "Times New Roman", 
           size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 0.97, y = 0.15,
           label = "US-aligned", family = "Times New Roman",
           size = 3, color = "grey40") +
  annotate("text", x = 0.97, y = -0.15,
           label = "Non-aligned", family = "Times New Roman",
           size = 3, color = "grey40") +
  labs(x     = "Mean Polyarchy Score",
       y     = "Mean Ideal Point (Bailey-Voeten)",
       title = "AIGOs Hold More Illiberal Preferences in Multilateral Settings",
       color = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(text          = element_text(family = "Times New Roman", size = 12),
        legend.position = "none")
```


# Results Visualization
```{r}
library(modelsummary)
library(kableExtra)
library(ggplot2)
library(sandwich)
library(clubSandwich)

cm <- c('gdp_cap' = 'GDP Per Capita', 
        'population' = 'Population',
        'trade' = 'Trade', 
        'globalization' = 'Globalization', 
        'alliances' = 'Alliances',
        'colonial' = 'Colonial',
        'numokay ber' = 'Number',
        'political' = 'Political',
        'social' = 'Social',
        'hh_gdp'= 'Economy HHI',
        'hh_poly' = 'Polyarchy HHI',
        'ideal_sd'='Preference Heterogeneity',
        'polyarchy_sd' = 'Political Heterogeneity', 
        'percentage' = 'Percentage',
        'polyarchy_median' = 'Polyarchy Median',
        'polyarchy' = 'Polyarchy')

#Pooling
models <- list("Baseline"=model0, "Polyarchy Median"=model1, "Polyarchy Mean"=model2, "Percentage"=model3) 


library(kableExtra)

modelsummary(models,
  stars = TRUE,
  coef_omit = "Interc",
  output = "latex",
  title = "Regression Results",
  notes = list("+ p < 0.1; * p < 0.05; ** p < 0.01; *** p < 0.001"),
  booktabs = TRUE,
  escape = FALSE,
  fmt = 3
) %>%
  save_kable("pooling_table_clean.tex")


library(modelsummary)
library(ggplot2)

pooling_result <- modelplot(models, coef_omit = 'Interc', coef_map = cm) +
   theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("grey50","grey30", "grey10", "black"))+
  theme(text = element_text(family = "Times New Roman", size = 14))


ggsave("pooling_result.pdf", plot = pooling_result, width = 6, height = 4)

```
